---
title: "Analysing Data of Zika and Chikungunya Cases from 2015 to 2025 in Brazil"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Importing the Data

```{r}
library(read.dbc)

CHIK15 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR15.dbc")
CHIK16 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR16.dbc")
CHIK17 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR17.dbc")
CHIK18 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR18.dbc")
CHIK19 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR19.dbc")
CHIK20 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR20.dbc")
CHIK21 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR21.dbc")
CHIK22 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR22.dbc")
CHIK23 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR23.dbc")
CHIK24 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKdata/CHIKBR24.dbc")
CHIK25 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/CHIKBR25.dbc")

ZIKA16 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/zikadata/ZIKABR16.dbc")
ZIKA17 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/zikadata/ZIKABR17.dbc")
ZIKA18 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/zikadata/ZIKABR18.dbc")
ZIKA19 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/zikadata/ZIKABR19.dbc")
ZIKA20 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/zikadata/ZIKABR20.dbc")
ZIKA21 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/zikadata/ZIKABR21.dbc")
ZIKA22 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/zikadata/ZIKABR22.dbc")
ZIKA23 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/zikadata/ZIKABR23.dbc")
ZIKA24 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/zikadata/ZIKABR24.dbc")
ZIKA25 <- read.dbc("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/ZIKABR25.dbc")
```

Combining The Data

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ==== 1. Import and combine raw data ====
chik_list <- list(CHIK15, CHIK16, CHIK17, CHIK18, CHIK19, CHIK20, CHIK21, CHIK22, CHIK23, CHIK24, CHIK25)
zika_list <- list(ZIKA16, ZIKA17, ZIKA18, ZIKA19, ZIKA20, ZIKA21, ZIKA22, ZIKA23, ZIKA24, ZIKA25)

Chik_total <- bind_rows(chik_list)
Zika_total <- bind_rows(zika_list)

# ==== 2. Convert date columns and extract year ====
Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI <- as.Date(Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI)
Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI <- as.Date(Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI)

Chik_total$year_all <- as.numeric(format(Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y"))
Zika_total$year_all <- as.numeric(format(Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y"))

# ==== 3. Plot all-time case trend (using CLASSI_FIN == 13 for Chikungunya, 1 for Zika) ====
chik_cases_by_year <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), !is.na(year_all), year_all >= 1902, year_all <= 2025) %>%
  count(year_all) %>%
  mutate(virus = "Chikungunya")


zika_cases_by_year <- Zika_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN == 1, !is.na(year_all), year_all >= 1902, year_all <= 2025) %>%
  count(year_all) %>%
  mutate(virus = "Zika")

cases_by_year_all <- bind_rows(chik_cases_by_year, zika_cases_by_year)

ggplot(cases_by_year_all, aes(x = year_all, y = n, fill = virus)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1902, 2025, 5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Zika (1) and Chikungunya (13) Cases by Year (1902–2025)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Cases",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Bar chart showing annual confirmed case counts for Zika (blue) and Chikungunya (red) from 1902 to 2025. The plot highlights the absence of reported cases for most of the 20th century and the sharp rise in cases after 2015, corresponding to recent epidemic outbreaks in Brazil.


# ==== 4. Filter confirmed cases from 2015 to 2025 for further analysis ====
Chik_total <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1,13),
         !is.na(DT_SIN_PRI),
         DT_SIN_PRI >= as.Date("2015-01-01"),
         DT_SIN_PRI <= as.Date("2025-12-31"))

Zika_total <- Zika_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN == 1,
         !is.na(DT_SIN_PRI),
         DT_SIN_PRI >= as.Date("2015-01-01"),
         DT_SIN_PRI <= as.Date("2025-12-31"))

# ==== 5. Plot recent confirmed cases (2015–2025) ====
Chik_total$year <- format(Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y")
Zika_total$year <- format(Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y")

chik_recent_cases <- Chik_total %>%
  count(year) %>%
  mutate(virus = "Chikungunya")

zika_recent_cases <- Zika_total %>%
  count(year) %>%
  mutate(virus = "Zika")

cases_recent <- bind_rows(chik_recent_cases, zika_recent_cases)

ggplot(cases_recent, aes(x = year, y = n, fill = virus)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Confirmed Zika and Chikungunya Cases (2015–2025)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Confirmed Cases",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Bar chart comparing annual confirmed case counts for Zika (blue) and Chikungunya (red) between 2015 and 2025. The plot highlights the peak of the Zika epidemic in 2016, followed by consistently higher Chikungunya case counts in subsequent years.

# year-month 
Zika_total$month <- format(as.Date(Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI), "%m")
Zika_total$year_month <- format(as.Date(Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y-%m")

Chik_total$month <- format(as.Date(Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI), "%m")
Chik_total$year_month <- format(as.Date(Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y-%m")

zika_monthly <- Zika_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN == 1) %>%
  group_by(year = format(DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y"), month = format(DT_SIN_PRI, "%m")) %>%
  summarise(cases = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(virus = "Zika")

chik_monthly <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13)) %>%
  group_by(year = format(DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y"), month = format(DT_SIN_PRI, "%m")) %>%
  summarise(cases = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(virus = "Chikungunya")


monthly_combined <- bind_rows(zika_monthly, chik_monthly)

monthly_combined$month <- factor(monthly_combined$month, 
                                  levels = sprintf("%02d", 1:12), 
                                  labels = month.abb, 
                                  ordered = TRUE)

ggplot(monthly_combined, aes(x = month, y = cases, fill = virus)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ year, ncol = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")) +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Confirmed Cases of Zika and Chikungunya (Seasonal Trends)",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of Confirmed Cases",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14)
  )

# Faceted bar charts showing monthly confirmed case counts for Zika (green) and Chikungunya (orange) from 2015 to 2025. The plots reveal clear seasonal patterns, with peaks typically occurring in March, April or May. The Zika epidemic surge is concentrated in early 2016, while Chikungunya exhibits recurring seasonal outbreaks in multiple years.

```




Analysing the Severity of Zika and Chikungunya (Deaths)
The cases are marked follow the "Documento de Requisitos" for Dengue/Chikungunya
1-Cura
2- Óbito por Zika/Chikungunya
3- Óbito por outras causas
4 – Óbito em investigação
9- Ignorado 

```{r}

# Make sure the Date format（Start from Symptoms occur）
Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI <- as.Date(Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI)
Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI <- as.Date(Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI)

# Extract Yearss
Chik_total$year <- format(Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y")
Zika_total$year <- format(Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y")

# Filter the death cases（Caused by zika or chik）
chik_death <- Chik_total %>% filter(EVOLUCAO == 2)
zika_death <- Zika_total %>% filter(EVOLUCAO == 2)

# Death Cases by years
chik_death_yearly <- chik_death %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(deaths = n()) %>%
  mutate(virus = "Chikungunya")

zika_death_yearly <- zika_death %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(deaths = n()) %>%
  mutate(virus = "Zika")

# Combine the data
death_summary <- bind_rows(chik_death_yearly, zika_death_yearly)


#Years+ Death cases
full_years <- sort(unique(c(chik_death_yearly$year, zika_death_yearly$year)))
full_combo <- expand.grid(
  year = full_years,
  virus = c("Zika", "Chikungunya")
)

death_summary_complete <- full_combo %>%
  left_join(death_summary, by = c("year", "virus")) %>%
  mutate(deaths = ifelse(is.na(deaths), 0, deaths))


#Combined figure
ggplot(death_summary_complete, aes(x = year, y = deaths, fill = virus)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual Deaths Caused by Zika and Chikungunya (EVOLUCAO = 2)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Deaths",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Annual deaths caused by Zika and Chikungunya in Brazil (2015–2025). Chikungunya (orange) accounts for the vast majority of deaths across the period, with a pronounced peak in 2016 (~185 deaths) followed by another high in 2017 (~125 deaths) and a resurgence in 2024 (~135 deaths). Zika (green) deaths are comparatively rare, peaking in 2016 (~45 deaths) and dropping to single digits in most other years.


# Death by month~year

chik_death_monthly <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(EVOLUCAO == 2) %>%
  mutate(
    year = format(DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y"),
    month = format(DT_SIN_PRI, "%m"),
    month = factor(month, levels = sprintf("%02d", 1:12), labels = month.abb, ordered = TRUE),
    virus = "Chikungunya"
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, month, virus) %>%
  summarise(deaths = n(), .groups = "drop")

zika_death_monthly <- Zika_total %>%
  filter(EVOLUCAO == 2) %>%
  mutate(
    year = format(DT_SIN_PRI, "%Y"),
    month = format(DT_SIN_PRI, "%m"),
    month = factor(month, levels = sprintf("%02d", 1:12), labels = month.abb, ordered = TRUE),
    virus = "Zika"
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, month, virus) %>%
  summarise(deaths = n(), .groups = "drop")


death_monthly_combined <- bind_rows(chik_death_monthly, zika_death_monthly)


ggplot(death_monthly_combined, aes(x = month, y = deaths, fill = virus)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ year, ncol = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")) +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Deaths by Virus (Zika vs Chikungunya)",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of Deaths",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

# Monthly deaths by Zika and Chikungunya in Brazil (2015–2025). Chikungunya (orange) shows pronounced seasonal mortality peaks, most notably in early 2016 (Feb–Apr, >35 deaths/month), early 2017 (Feb–Mar, >40 deaths), and early 2024 (Feb, ~32 deaths). Smaller surges appear in 2019, 2022, and 2023. Zika (green) mortality is minimal, with a visible but modest rise in early 2016 (~8–10 deaths/month) and isolated small counts in other years. Deaths for both viruses are concentrated in the first half of outbreak years, indicating strong seasonality.


```


Age distribution of all/confirmed/discarded/suspected cases in Zika and Chikungunya.

The age is recorded by 4 digits, the first digit follow the "Documento de Requisitos" 
1. Hour
2. Day
3. Month
4. Year

```{r}

# 1. Categorize case status
Chik_total <- Chik_total %>%
  mutate(case_status = case_when(
    EVOLUCAO == 2 ~ "Death",
    CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13) ~ "Confirmed",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

Zika_total <- Zika_total %>%
  mutate(case_status = case_when(
    EVOLUCAO == 2 ~ "Death",
    CLASSI_FIN == 1 ~ "Confirmed",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# 2. Decode age
decode_age_years <- function(NU_IDADE_N) {
  unit_code <- floor(NU_IDADE_N / 1000)
  value <- NU_IDADE_N %% 1000
  dplyr::case_when(
    unit_code == 1 ~ value / (24 * 365),  # Hours
    unit_code == 2 ~ value / 365,         # Days
    unit_code == 3 ~ value / 12,          # Months
    unit_code == 4 ~ value,               # Years
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )
}

Chik_total$age_years <- decode_age_years(Chik_total$NU_IDADE_N)
Zika_total$age_years <- decode_age_years(Zika_total$NU_IDADE_N)

# 3. Add virus label and combine datasets
Chik_total$virus <- "Chikungunya"
Zika_total$virus <- "Zika"
combined_all <- bind_rows(Chik_total, Zika_total)

# 4. Load ggplot2
library(ggplot2)

# ==== Plot 1: Age distribution of confirmed cases only ====
ggplot(
  combined_all %>% 
    filter(case_status == "Confirmed", !is.na(age_years), age_years <= 120),
  aes(x = age_years, fill = virus)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "identity", alpha = 0.6, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")) +
  labs(
    title = "Age Distribution of Confirmed Zika and Chikungunya Cases",
    x = "Age (years)",
    y = "Number of Cases",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ==== Plot 2: Age distribution of death cases only ====
ggplot(
  combined_all %>% 
    filter(case_status == "Death", !is.na(age_years), age_years <= 120),
  aes(x = age_years, fill = virus)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "identity", alpha = 0.6, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")) +
  labs(
    title = "Age Distribution of Deaths Caused by Zika and Chikungunya",
    x = "Age (years)",
    y = "Number of Deaths",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#  Add age group column (run this BEFORE the stacked bar plots)
combined_all <- combined_all %>%
  mutate(age_group = case_when(
    age_years >= 0 & age_years < 1    ~ "< 1",
    age_years >= 1 & age_years < 5    ~ "1–4",
    age_years >= 5 & age_years < 10   ~ "5–9",
    age_years >= 10 & age_years < 15  ~ "10–14",
    age_years >= 15 & age_years < 20  ~ "15–19",
    age_years >= 20 & age_years < 25  ~ "20–24",
    age_years >= 25 & age_years < 30  ~ "25–29",
    age_years >= 30 & age_years < 35  ~ "30–34",
    age_years >= 35 & age_years < 40  ~ "35–39",
    age_years >= 40 & age_years < 45  ~ "40–44",
    age_years >= 45 & age_years < 50  ~ "45–49",
    age_years >= 50 & age_years < 55  ~ "50–54",
    age_years >= 55 & age_years < 60  ~ "55–59",
    age_years >= 60 & age_years < 65  ~ "60–64",
    age_years >= 65 & age_years < 70  ~ "65–69",
    age_years >= 70 & age_years < 75  ~ "70–74",
    age_years >= 75 & age_years < 80  ~ "75–79",
    age_years >= 80 & age_years < 85  ~ "80–84",
    age_years >= 85 & age_years < 90  ~ "85–89",
    age_years >= 90 & age_years < 95  ~ "90–94",
    age_years >= 95 & age_years < 100 ~ "95–99",
    age_years >= 100 & age_years <= 120 ~ "100+",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))
# Reorder age_group as a factor with correct levels
age_levels <- c(
  "< 1","1–4", "5–9", "10–14", "15–19", "20–24", "25–29", "30–34", "35–39",
  "40–44", "45–49", "50–54", "55–59", "60–64", "65–69", "70–74", "75–79",
  "80–84", "85–89", "90–94", "95–99", "100+"
)

combined_all <- combined_all %>%
  mutate(age_group = factor(age_group, levels = age_levels, ordered = TRUE))


#  Age Group Stacked Bar: Confirmed Cases (%)
confirmed_pct <- combined_all %>%
  filter(case_status == "Confirmed", !is.na(age_group)) %>%
  group_by(virus, age_group) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(virus) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 2))

ggplot(confirmed_pct, aes(x = virus, y = percentage, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Age Group Composition of Confirmed Cases (%)",
    x = "Virus",
    y = "Percentage of Confirmed Cases",
    fill = "Age Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


#  Age Group Stacked Bar: Deaths (%)
death_pct <- combined_all %>%
  filter(case_status == "Death", !is.na(age_group)) %>%
  group_by(virus, age_group) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(virus) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 2))

ggplot(death_pct, aes(x = virus, y = percentage, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Age Group Composition of Deaths (%)",
    x = "Virus",
    y = "Percentage of Deaths",
    fill = "Age Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


#CFR

confirmed_all <- combined_all %>%
  filter(
    (
      virus == "Chikungunya" & CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1,13)
    ) |
    (
      virus == "Zika" & CLASSI_FIN == 1
    ),
    !is.na(age_group)
  )

cfr_by_age <- confirmed_all %>%
  group_by(age_group, virus) %>%
  summarise(
    confirmed = n(),
    deaths = sum(EVOLUCAO == 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    CFR = round(100 * deaths / confirmed, 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(cfr_by_age,
       aes(x = age_group, y = CFR, fill = virus)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Case Fatality Rate by Age Group",
    x = "Age Group",
    y = "CFR (%)",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#race by year cases
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2)

combined_all$year <- format(as.Date(combined_all$DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y")

age_year_summary <- combined_all %>%
  filter(case_status == "Confirmed",
         !is.na(age_group),
         year >= "2015", year <= "2025") %>%
  group_by(year, virus, age_group) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(year, virus) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 2)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(age_year_summary, aes(x = year, y = percentage, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~ virus) +
  labs(
    title = "Age-Group Composition per Year (2015–2025, Confirmed Only)",
    x = "Year", y = "Percentage of Confirmed Cases", fill = "Age Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))



```



wavelet analysis
```{r}


# ============================================================
# 0. Libraries
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(WaveletComp)

# ============================================================
# 1. Create National Monthly Time Series (2015–2025)
# ============================================================

monthly_cases <- combined_all %>%
  filter(
    virus == "Chikungunya",
    !is.na(DT_NOTIFIC)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(DT_NOTIFIC),
    year = year(date),
    month = month(date)
  ) %>%
  filter(year >= 2015, year <= 2025) %>%
  group_by(year, month) %>%
  summarise(cases = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(year, month)

# Fill missing months with 0
all_months <- data.frame(
  year = rep(2015:2025, each = 12),
  month = rep(1:12, times = length(2015:2025))
)

monthly_cases <- all_months %>%
  left_join(monthly_cases, by = c("year", "month")) %>%
  mutate(cases = ifelse(is.na(cases), 0, cases)) %>%
  arrange(year, month)

# ============================================================
# 2. Wavelet Transform
# ============================================================

wavelet_data <- data.frame(
  time = 1:nrow(monthly_cases),
  cases = monthly_cases$cases
)

wt <- analyze.wavelet(
  wavelet_data,
  my.series = "cases",
  loess.span = 0,
  dt = 1/12,
  dj = 1/20,
  lowerPeriod = 2/12,
  upperPeriod = 5,
  make.pval = TRUE,
  n.sim = 200
)

# ============================================================
# 3. Create Publication-Ready Scalogram
# ============================================================

png("Wavelet_Scalogram_Chikungunya_2015_2025.png",
    width = 1600, height = 1200, res = 200)

wt.image(
  wt,
  color.key = "interval",
  n.levels = 250,
  legend.params = list(lab = "Wavelet Power"),
  show.date = FALSE,
  periodlab = "Period (Years)",
  timelab = "Month",
  main = "Wavelet Power Spectrum of Monthly Chikungunya Cases\nBrazil, 2015–2025",
  siglvl = 0.05,
)

# Custom x-axis with real years
axis(
  side = 1,
  at = seq(1, nrow(monthly_cases), by = 12),
  labels = 2015:2025
)

dev.off()

# ============================================================
# 4. Global Wavelet Spectrum (Optional but Included)
# ============================================================

png("Global_Wavelet_Spectrum_Chikungunya_2015_2025.png",
    width = 1000, height = 800, res = 200)

wt.avg(
  wt,
  main = "Global Wavelet Spectrum\nBrazil 2015–2025"
)

dev.off()

# ============================================================
# 5. Save Wavelet Object
# ============================================================

saveRDS(wt, "wavelet_result_2015_2025.rds")

```



Demographics distribution of Zika/Chikungunya cases
The cases are marked follow the "Documento de Requisitos"
1- branca
2- preta
3- amarela
4- parda
5- indígena
9 Ignorado 

```{r}


# Demographics Distribution – Confirmed Cases Only (CLASSI_FIN 1,13)
race_dist_by_virus <- combined_all %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), !is.na(CS_RACA)) %>%
  group_by(virus, CS_RACA) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    race_label = case_when(
      CS_RACA == 1 ~ "White",
      CS_RACA == 2 ~ "Black",
      CS_RACA == 3 ~ "Asian",
      CS_RACA == 4 ~ "Pardo (Mixed)",
      CS_RACA == 5 ~ "Indigenous",
      CS_RACA == 9 ~ "Unknown",
      TRUE ~ "Unspecified"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(virus) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 2))

ggplot(race_dist_by_virus, aes(x = race_label, y = n, fill = race_label)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~ virus) +
  labs(
    title = "Demographics Distribution by Virus (Confirmed Cases Only)",
    x = "Demographics",
    y = "Number of Confirmed Cases"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")


# Demographics Distribution Over Time (Confirmed Cases Only)
combined_all$year <- format(as.Date(combined_all$DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y")

race_year_summary <- combined_all %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), year >= "2015", year <= "2025", !is.na(CS_RACA)) %>%
  group_by(year, virus, CS_RACA) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    race_label = case_when(
      CS_RACA == 1 ~ "White",
      CS_RACA == 2 ~ "Black",
      CS_RACA == 3 ~ "Asian",
      CS_RACA == 4 ~ "Pardo (Mixed)",
      CS_RACA == 5 ~ "Indigenous",
      CS_RACA == 9 ~ "Unknown",
      TRUE ~ "Unspecified"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, virus) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 2)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(race_year_summary, aes(x = year, y = percentage, fill = race_label)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~ virus) +
  labs(
    title = "Ethnicity Distribution per Year (2015–2025, Confirmed Only)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Percentage of Confirmed Cases",
    fill = "Demographics"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


# Race vs Deaths (EVOLUCAO == 2)
death_race_summary <- combined_all %>%
  filter(EVOLUCAO == 2, !is.na(CS_RACA)) %>%
  group_by(virus, CS_RACA) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    race_label = case_when(
      CS_RACA == 1 ~ "White",
      CS_RACA == 2 ~ "Black",
      CS_RACA == 3 ~ "Asian",
      CS_RACA == 4 ~ "Pardo (Mixed)",
      CS_RACA == 5 ~ "Indigenous",
      CS_RACA == 9 ~ "Unknown",
      TRUE ~ "Unspecified"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(virus) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 2)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(death_race_summary, aes(x = race_label, y = n, fill = race_label)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~ virus) +
  labs(
    title = "Demographics Distribution among Deaths (EVOLUCAO = 2)",
    x = "Demographics",
    y = "Number of Deaths"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")


# Case Fatality Rate by Demographics
race_case_total <- combined_all %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), !is.na(CS_RACA)) %>%
  group_by(virus, CS_RACA) %>%
  summarise(total_cases = n(), .groups = "drop")

race_death_total <- combined_all %>%
  filter(EVOLUCAO == 2, !is.na(CS_RACA)) %>%
  group_by(virus, CS_RACA) %>%
  summarise(deaths = n(), .groups = "drop")

race_cfr <- left_join(race_case_total, race_death_total, by = c("virus", "CS_RACA")) %>%
  mutate(
    deaths = ifelse(is.na(deaths), 0, deaths),
    CFR = round(deaths / total_cases * 100, 2),
    race_label = case_when(
      CS_RACA == 1 ~ "White",
      CS_RACA == 2 ~ "Black",
      CS_RACA == 3 ~ "Asian",
      CS_RACA == 4 ~ "Pardo (Mixed)",
      CS_RACA == 5 ~ "Indigenous",
      CS_RACA == 9 ~ "Unknown",
      TRUE ~ "Unspecified"
    )
  )

ggplot(
  race_cfr %>% filter(CS_RACA %in% 1:5),
  aes(x = race_label, y = CFR, fill = race_label)
) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~ virus) +
  labs(
    title = "Case Fatality Rate by Demographics (Confirmed Cases Only, EVOLUCAO = 2)",
    x = "Demographics",
    y = "Case Fatality Rate (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")


# ==== Overall CFR for Zika and Chikungunya ====
overall_confirmed <- combined_all %>%
  filter(case_status == "Confirmed") %>%
  count(virus, name = "total_cases")

overall_deaths <- combined_all %>%
  filter(case_status == "Death") %>%
  count(virus, name = "deaths")

overall_cfr <- left_join(overall_confirmed, overall_deaths, by = "virus") %>%
  mutate(
    deaths = ifelse(is.na(deaths), 0, deaths),
    CFR = round((deaths / total_cases) * 100, 2)
  )

print(overall_cfr)

# Optional: Visualize overall CFR
ggplot(overall_cfr, aes(x = virus, y = CFR, fill = virus)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Overall Case Fatality Rate by Virus (2015–2025)",
    x = "Virus",
    y = "CFR (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

Geographical mapping
References: https://www.ibge.gov.br/explica/codigos-dos-municipios.php

```{r}
# === Required libraries ===
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(geobr)
library(RColorBrewer)

# === Load Brazilian state shapefile and join with population and density ===
brazil_states <- read_state(code_state = "all", year = 2020, showProgress = FALSE)

state_population <- tibble::tibble(
  abbrev_state = c("SP", "MG", "RJ", "BA", "PR", "RS", "PE", "CE", "PA", "SC",
                   "GO", "MA", "AM", "PB", "ES", "MT", "RN", "PI", "AL", "DF",
                   "MS", "SE", "RO", "TO", "AC", "AP", "RR"),
  population = c(45973190, 21322690, 17219680, 14850510, 11824670, 11229920, 
                 9539030, 9233660, 8664310, 8058440, 7350480, 7010960, 4281210, 
                 4145040, 4102130, 3836400, 3446070, 3375650, 3220100, 2982820, 
                 2901900, 2291080, 1746230, 1577340, 880630, 802840, 716790),
  density = c(6.34, 125.52, 2.63, 2.58, 30.52, 60.33, 493.00, 80.63, 18.46, 19.03,
              4.01, 7.83, 31.72, 7.02, 78.93, 43.46, 103.83, 9.73, 387.46, 62.74,
              36.84, 7.34, 2.54, 69.74, 175.73, 97.64, 5.74)
)

brazil_states <- brazil_states %>%
  left_join(state_population, by = "abbrev_state")

# === Prepare Year and State Code in Disease Data ===
Zika_total$year <- format(as.Date(Zika_total$DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y")
Zika_total$SG_UF_NOT <- as.integer(Zika_total$SG_UF_NOT)

Chik_total$year <- format(as.Date(Chik_total$DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y")
Chik_total$SG_UF_NOT <- as.integer(Chik_total$SG_UF_NOT)

# === Count Confirmed Cases by Year and State ===
zika_by_state_year <- Zika_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN == 1, year %in% as.character(2015:2025)) %>%
  group_by(year, SG_UF_NOT) %>%
  summarise(cases = n(), .groups = "drop")

chik_by_state_year <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), year %in% as.character(2015:2025)) %>%
  group_by(year, SG_UF_NOT) %>%
  summarise(cases = n(), .groups = "drop")


# === Build Map Data ===
map_data_zika_yearly <- expand.grid(
  year = as.character(2015:2025),
  code_state = brazil_states$code_state
) %>%
  mutate(SG_UF_NOT = as.integer(code_state)) %>%
  left_join(brazil_states, by = "code_state") %>%
  left_join(zika_by_state_year, by = c("SG_UF_NOT", "year")) %>%
  mutate(
    cases = ifelse(is.na(cases), 0, cases),
    incidence_per_100 = round((cases / population) * 100, 4),
    burden_incidence_per_density = round(incidence_per_100 / density, 4)
  )

map_data_chik_yearly <- expand.grid(
  year = as.character(2015:2025),
  code_state = brazil_states$code_state
) %>%
  mutate(SG_UF_NOT = as.integer(code_state)) %>%
  left_join(brazil_states, by = "code_state") %>%
  left_join(chik_by_state_year, by = c("SG_UF_NOT", "year")) %>%
  mutate(
    cases = ifelse(is.na(cases), 0, cases),
    incidence_per_100 = round((cases / population) * 100, 4),
    burden_incidence_per_density = round(incidence_per_100 / density, 4)
  )



# Using the geobr package-->6 municipality code
muni_sf <- read_municipality(year = 2020) %>%
  mutate(code_muni6 = substr(as.character(code_muni), 1, 6))


Zika_total <- Zika_total %>%
  mutate(
    year = format(as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y"),
    code_muni6 = substr(as.character(ID_MUNICIP), 1, 6)
  )

Chik_total <- Chik_total %>%
  mutate(
    year = format(as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y"),
    code_muni6 = substr(as.character(ID_MUNICIP), 1, 6)
  )

#Count the case number by year and muni code
zika_muni <- Zika_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN == 1, year %in% as.character(2015:2025)) %>%
  group_by(year, code_muni6) %>%
  summarise(cases = n(), .groups = "drop")

chik_muni <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), year %in% as.character(2015:2025)) %>%
  group_by(year, code_muni6) %>%
  summarise(cases = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Match wit the updated 7 digits code
muni_lookup <- muni_sf %>%
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
  dplyr::select(code_muni, code_muni6)

zika_muni <- zika_muni %>%
  left_join(muni_lookup, by = "code_muni6") %>%
  mutate(code_muni = as.numeric(code_muni))

chik_muni <- chik_muni %>%
  left_join(muni_lookup, by = "code_muni6") %>%
  mutate(code_muni = as.numeric(code_muni))
#combine all data together
map_data_zika <- expand.grid(
  year = as.character(2015:2025),
  code_muni = unique(muni_sf$code_muni)
) %>%
  left_join(muni_sf, by = "code_muni") %>%
  left_join(zika_muni, by = c("code_muni", "year")) %>%
  mutate(cases = ifelse(is.na(cases), 0, cases))

map_data_chik <- expand.grid(
  year = as.character(2015:2025),
  code_muni = unique(muni_sf$code_muni)
) %>%
  left_join(muni_sf, by = "code_muni") %>%
  left_join(chik_muni, by = c("code_muni", "year")) %>%
  mutate(cases = ifelse(is.na(cases), 0, cases))

#Population by Municipality
library(readxl)
df <- read_excel("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/POP2024_20241230.xlsx")

df$`COD. UF` <- as.integer(df$`COD. UF`)
df$`COD. MUNIC` <- as.integer(df$`COD. MUNIC`)
df$code_muni <- sprintf("%02d%05d", df$`COD. UF`, df$`COD. MUNIC`)
map_data_zika$code_muni <- as.character(map_data_zika$code_muni)
df$code_muni <- as.character(df$code_muni)
map_data_chik$code_muni <- as.character(map_data_chik$code_muni)


map_data_zika <- map_data_zika %>%
  left_join(dplyr::select(df, code_muni, `POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`), by = "code_muni") %>%
  mutate(
    incidence_per_100k = round((cases / `POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`) * 100000, 2)
  )

map_data_zika <- map_data_zika %>%
  mutate(
    incidence_per_1k = round((cases / `POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`) * 1000, 2)
  )

# Chikungunya incidence 
map_data_chik <- map_data_chik %>%
  left_join(dplyr::select(df, code_muni, `POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`), by = "code_muni") %>%
  mutate(
    incidence_per_100k = round((cases / `POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`) * 100000, 2)
  )

map_data_chik <- map_data_chik %>%
  mutate(
    incidence_per_1k = round((cases / `POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`) * 1000, 2)
  )


states   <- read_state(year = 2020)                           
regions  <- read_region(year = 2020)                            
                  
big_cities <- read_municipality(year = 2020) %>%
  filter(name_muni %in% c("São Paulo", "Rio de Janeiro", "Salvador",
                          "Belo Horizonte", "Fortaleza", "Manaus",
                          "Recife", "Porto Alegre", "Brasília", "Curitiba")) %>%
  mutate(centroid = st_point_on_surface(geom)) %>%
  st_as_sf() %>% st_set_geometry("centroid")


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(viridis)

# —— Chik incidence interval——
inc_breaks_chik <- c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, Inf)
inc_labels_chik <- c("0–0.5","0.5–1","1–2","2–5","5–10","10–20","20–50","50+")

# —— Zika incidence interval ——
inc_breaks_zika <- c(0, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, Inf)
inc_labels_zika <- c("0–0.1","0.1–0.25","0.25–0.5","0.5–1","1–2","2–5","5–10","10+")

bin_incidence_custom <- function(df, col, breaks, labels){
  df |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      inc_bin = cut(.data[[col]], breaks = breaks, labels = labels,
                    include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE)
    )
}

# --- Chik ---
chik_binned <- bin_incidence_custom(map_data_chik, "incidence_per_1k",
                                    inc_breaks_chik, inc_labels_chik)
chik_labels <- levels(chik_binned$inc_bin)
chik_cols   <- c("grey80", viridis::viridis(length(chik_labels)-1, option = "C"))
names(chik_cols) <- chik_labels


# --- Zika ---
zika_binned <- bin_incidence_custom(map_data_zika, "incidence_per_1k",
                                    inc_breaks_zika, inc_labels_zika)
zika_labels <- levels(zika_binned$inc_bin)
zika_cols   <- c("grey80", viridis::viridis(length(zika_labels)-1, option = "C"))
names(zika_cols) <- zika_labels


# Chik：0, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50  (/1k)
inc_breaks_chik_100k <- c(0, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000, Inf)
inc_labels_chik_100k <- c("0–50", "50–100", "100–200", "200–500",
                          "500–1,000", "1,000–2,000", "2,000–5,000", "5,000+")

# Zika：0, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10  (/1k)
inc_breaks_zika_100k <- c(0, 10, 25, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, Inf)
inc_labels_zika_100k <- c("0–10", "10–25", "25–50", "50–100",
                          "100–200", "200–500", "500–1,000", "1,000+")

bin_incidence_custom <- function(df, col, breaks, labels){
  df |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      inc_bin = cut(.data[[col]], breaks = breaks, labels = labels,
                    include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE)
    )
}

# --- Chik per-100k ---
chik_binned_100k <- bin_incidence_custom(
  map_data_chik, "incidence_per_100k",
  inc_breaks_chik_100k, inc_labels_chik_100k
)
chik_labels_100k <- levels(chik_binned_100k$inc_bin)
chik_cols_100k   <- c("grey80", viridis::viridis(length(chik_labels_100k) - 1, option = "C"))
names(chik_cols_100k) <- chik_labels_100k

ggplot(chik_binned_100k) +
  geom_sf(aes(geometry = geom, fill = inc_bin), color = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~ year, ncol = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = chik_cols_100k, name = "Incidence", drop = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Chikungunya Incidence by Municipality (per 100k, 2015–2025)") + theme_minimal()

# --- Zika per-100k ---
zika_binned_100k <- bin_incidence_custom(
  map_data_zika, "incidence_per_100k",
  inc_breaks_zika_100k, inc_labels_zika_100k
)
zika_labels_100k <- levels(zika_binned_100k$inc_bin)
zika_cols_100k   <- c("grey80", viridis::viridis(length(zika_labels_100k) - 1, option = "C"))
names(zika_cols_100k) <- zika_labels_100k

ggplot(zika_binned_100k) +
  geom_sf(aes(geometry = geom, fill = inc_bin), color = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~ year, ncol = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = zika_cols_100k, name = "Incidence rate (Cases per 100k people)", drop = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Zika Incidence by Municipality (per 100k, 2015–2025)") + theme_minimal()


```




Hospitalization-Chikungunya
1-Yes
2-No
3-Unknown

```{r}
library(scales)
Chik_total$HOSPITALIZ <- as.character(Chik_total$HOSPITALIZ)

chik_hosp_summary <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), HOSPITALIZ %in% c("1", "2")) %>%
  count(HOSPITALIZ) %>%
  mutate(
    hosp_status = ifelse(HOSPITALIZ == "1", "Hospitalized", "Not Hospitalized")
  ) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 2))


ggplot(chik_hosp_summary, aes(x = hosp_status, y = percentage, fill = hosp_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Hospitalization Rate for Confirmed Chikungunya Cases",
    x = "Hospitalization Status",
    y = "Percentage of Cases"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")


# === delay model===
combined_all <- combined_all %>%
  mutate(
    report_delay = as.numeric(as.Date(DT_NOTIFIC) - as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI)),
    death_delay  = as.numeric(as.Date(DT_OBITO) - as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI)),
    hosp_delay   = as.numeric(as.Date(DT_INTERNA) - as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    is.na(report_delay) | report_delay >= 0,
    is.na(death_delay)  | death_delay >= 0,
    is.na(hosp_delay)   | hosp_delay >= 0
  )

# === 1. report delay ===
ggplot(combined_all %>% filter(!is.na(report_delay), report_delay < 50), 
       aes(x = report_delay, fill = virus)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, alpha = 0.6, color = "black", position = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")) +
  labs(
    title = "Delay from Symptom Onset to Notification",
    x = "Delay (days)",
    y = "Number of Cases",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# === 2. death delay report ===
ggplot(combined_all %>% filter(case_status == "Death", !is.na(death_delay),death_delay < 50), 
       aes(x = death_delay, fill = virus)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, alpha = 0.6,color="black", position = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")) +
  labs(
    title = "Delay from Symptom Onset to Death",
    x = "Delay (days)",
    y = "Number of Deaths",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# === 3. Hosp delay report（ Chikungunya only）===
ggplot(combined_all %>% filter(virus == "Chikungunya", HOSPITALIZ == "1", !is.na(hosp_delay), hosp_delay < 50), 
       aes(x = hosp_delay)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1,color= "black", fill = "#fc8d62") +
  labs(
    title = "Delay from Symptom Onset to Hospitalization (Chikungunya)",
    x = "Delay (days)",
    y = "Number of Hospitalized Cases"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# === 4. death delay + geo (Municipality) ===

muni_sf <- read_municipality(year = 2020, showProgress = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(code_muni6 = substr(as.character(code_muni), 1, 6))


all_muni_virus <- expand.grid(
  code_muni6 = unique(muni_sf$code_muni6),
  virus = unique(combined_all$virus)
)

# calculate report delay
death_delay_muni <- combined_all %>%
  filter(case_status == "Death", !is.na(death_delay)) %>%
  mutate(code_muni6 = substr(as.character(ID_MUNICIP), 1, 6)) %>%
  group_by(code_muni6, virus) %>%
  summarise(mean_delay = mean(death_delay), .groups = "drop")


death_delay_map <- all_muni_virus %>%
  left_join(death_delay_muni, by = c("code_muni6", "virus")) %>%
  left_join(muni_sf, by = "code_muni6")


ggplot(death_delay_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(geometry = geom, fill = mean_delay), color = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~ virus) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name   = "Mean Delay (days)",
    limits = c(0, 50),
    breaks = seq(0, 50, 10),
    oob    = squish,      
    na.value = "lightgrey"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Mean Time of time lag between Symptom Onset to Death by Municipality from 2015 to 2025") +
  theme_minimal()


report_delay_muni <- combined_all %>%
  filter(!is.na(report_delay)) %>%
  mutate(code_muni6 = substr(as.character(ID_MUNICIP), 1, 6)) %>%
  group_by(code_muni6, virus) %>%
  summarise(mean_delay = mean(report_delay), .groups = "drop")


report_delay_map <- all_muni_virus %>%
  left_join(report_delay_muni, by = c("code_muni6", "virus")) %>%
  left_join(muni_sf, by = "code_muni6")


ggplot(report_delay_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(geometry = geom, fill = mean_delay), color = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~ virus) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name   = "Mean Delay (days)",
    limits = c(0, 50),
    breaks = seq(0, 50, 10),
    oob    = squish,     
    na.value = "lightgrey"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Mean Time of time lag between Symptom Onset to Notification by Municipality from 2015 to 2025") +
  theme_minimal()


suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(survival)
  library(survminer)
  library(scales)
})

df_report <- combined_all %>%
  filter(!is.na(report_delay), report_delay >= 0, report_delay <= 50)

sf_report <- survfit(Surv(report_delay, rep(1, nrow(df_report))) ~ virus, data = df_report)

ggsurvplot(
  sf_report,
  conf.int = FALSE, censor = FALSE,
  legend.title = "Virus",
  legend.labs = levels(factor(df_report$virus)),
  xlab = "Days since symptom onset",
  ylab = "P(delay > t)",
  title = "Survival Curve: Onset → Notification Delay",
  xlim = c(0, 50)
)

# —— 2)death delay（0–50days）——
df_death <- combined_all %>%
  filter(case_status == "Death", !is.na(death_delay), death_delay >= 0, death_delay <= 50)

sf_death <- survfit(Surv(death_delay, rep(1, nrow(df_death))) ~ virus, data = df_death)

ggsurvplot(
  sf_death,
  conf.int = FALSE, censor = FALSE,
  legend.title = "Virus",
  legend.labs = levels(factor(df_death$virus)),
  xlab = "Days since symptom onset",
  ylab = "P(delay > t)",
  title = "Survival Curve: Onset → Death Delay",
  xlim = c(0, 50)
)

# —— 3) hospitalization delay（0–50days）——
df_hosp <- combined_all %>%
  filter(virus == "Chikungunya", HOSPITALIZ == "1",
         !is.na(hosp_delay), hosp_delay >= 0, hosp_delay <= 50)

sf_hosp <- survfit(Surv(hosp_delay, rep(1, nrow(df_hosp))) ~ 1, data = df_hosp)

ggsurvplot(
  sf_hosp,
  conf.int = FALSE, censor = FALSE,
  legend = "none",
  xlab = "Days since symptom onset",
  ylab = "P(delay > t)",
  title = "Survival Curve: Onset → Hospitalization Delay (Chikungunya)",
  xlim = c(0, 50)
)
```


Age Distribution of Confirmed Cases
```{r}

#box plot age

combined_all$year <- factor(format(as.Date(combined_all$DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y"))


ggplot(
  combined_all %>% 
    filter(case_status == "Confirmed", !is.na(age_years), age_years <= 120),
  aes(x = year, y = age_years, fill = virus)
) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.8, outlier.alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual Age Distribution of Confirmed Cases (Box Plot)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Age (years)",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Annual age distribution of confirmed Zika and Chikungunya cases (2015–2025). Box plots show that Chikungunya (orange) cases have a consistently higher median age (~45–50 years) compared to Zika (green, ~30–40 years). For both viruses, the interquartile range is wide, spanning from young adults to elderly populations, with numerous outliers above 80 years. The distribution pattern remains stable across years, though slight increases in median age for Zika are visible after 2021.



ggplot(
  combined_all %>% 
    filter(case_status == "Death", !is.na(age_years), age_years <= 120),
  aes(x = year, y = age_years, fill = virus)
) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.8, outlier.alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual Age Distribution of Deaths (Box Plot)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Age (years)",
    fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Annual age distribution of deaths caused by Zika and Chikungunya (2015–2025). Chikungunya (orange) deaths occur predominantly among older adults, with median ages generally between 55–75 years and frequent cases in the 80+ range. In contrast, Zika (green) deaths are fewer but show distinct age patterns. Notably, 2015 and 2021 exhibit a concentration of infant deaths (age near 0), reflected in the extremely low medians for those years. Other Zika mortality years, such as 2016–2017, are dominated by young to middle-aged adults. Overall, Chikungunya mortality skews much older than Zika.


```


```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# =========================
# Prepare year variable
# =========================
combined_all <- combined_all %>%
  mutate(year = factor(format(as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y")))

# =========================
# Box plot: confirmed cases
# =========================
ggplot(
  combined_all %>%
    filter(
      virus == "Chikungunya",
      case_status == "Confirmed",
      !is.na(age_years),
      age_years <= 120
    ),
  aes(x = year, y = age_years)
) +
  geom_boxplot(
    fill = "#fc8d62",
    outlier.size = 0.8,
    outlier.alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual age distribution of confirmed chikungunya cases (2015–2025)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Age (years)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )



# Age by sex
ggplot(
  combined_all %>%
    filter(
      virus == "Chikungunya",
      case_status == "Confirmed",
      !is.na(age_years),
      age_years <= 120,
      CS_SEXO %in% c("M", "F")
    ),
  aes(x = year, y = age_years, fill = CS_SEXO)
) +
  geom_boxplot(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
    outlier.size = 0.5,
    outlier.alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("M" = "#66c2a5", "F" = "#fc8d62"),
    labels = c("M" = "Male", "F" = "Female")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual age distribution of confirmed chikungunya cases by sex (2015–2025)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Age (years)",
    fill = "Sex"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )




```



Survival analysis
```{r}


  library(dplyr)
  library(survival)
  library(survminer)
  library(broom)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)

combined_all <- combined_all %>%
  mutate(race_label = case_when(
    CS_RACA == 1 ~ "White",
    CS_RACA == 2 ~ "Black",
    CS_RACA == 3 ~ "Asian",
    CS_RACA == 4 ~ "Pardo (Mixed)",
    CS_RACA == 5 ~ "Indigenous",
    CS_RACA == 9 ~ "Unknown",
    TRUE ~ "Unspecified"
  ))


# Add a newborn-sensitive age grouping for survival
combined_all <- combined_all %>%
  mutate(age_group_surv = case_when(
    !is.na(age_years) & age_years < 1    ~ "<1",
    age_years >= 1  & age_years < 5      ~ "1–4",
    age_years >= 5  & age_years < 10     ~ "5–9",
    age_years >= 10 & age_years < 15     ~ "10–14",
    age_years >= 15 & age_years < 20     ~ "15–19",
    age_years >= 20 & age_years < 25     ~ "20–24",
    age_years >= 25 & age_years < 30     ~ "25–29",
    age_years >= 30 & age_years < 35     ~ "30–34",
    age_years >= 35 & age_years < 40     ~ "35–39",
    age_years >= 40 & age_years < 45     ~ "40–44",
    age_years >= 45 & age_years < 50     ~ "45–49",
    age_years >= 50 & age_years < 55     ~ "50–54",
    age_years >= 55 & age_years < 60     ~ "55–59",
    age_years >= 60 & age_years < 65     ~ "60–64",
    age_years >= 65 & age_years < 70     ~ "65–69",
    age_years >= 70 & age_years < 75     ~ "70–74",
    age_years >= 75 & age_years < 80     ~ "75–79",
    age_years >= 80 & age_years < 85     ~ "80–84",
    age_years >= 85 & age_years < 90     ~ "85–89",
    age_years >= 90 & age_years < 95     ~ "90–94",
    age_years >= 95 & age_years <= 120   ~ "95+",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ),
  age_group_surv = factor(age_group_surv,
    levels = c("<1","1–4","5–9","10–14","15–19","20–24","25–29","30–34",
               "35–39","40–44","45–49","50–54","55–59","60–64","65–69",
               "70–74","75–79","80–84","85–89","90–94","95+"),
    ordered = TRUE)
  )


build_surv_dataset <- function(df, virus_label, group_var,
                               time_event = "death_delay",
                               time_last_obs = "report_delay",
                               xlim_days = c(0, 50)) {
  df %>%
    filter(virus == virus_label) %>%
    mutate(
      status = ifelse(case_status == "Death" & !is.na(.data[[time_event]]), 1L, 0L),
      t_event = .data[[time_event]],
      t_censor_raw = .data[[time_last_obs]],
      t_censor_filled = ifelse(is.na(t_censor_raw), xlim_days[2], t_censor_raw),
      t_obs = ifelse(status == 1L, t_event, t_censor_filled),
      t_obs = pmin(pmax(t_obs, xlim_days[1]), xlim_days[2]),
      .grp = droplevels(factor(.data[[group_var]]))
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(t_obs), !is.na(.grp))
}

plot_km_by <- function(df, virus_label, group_var,
                       time_event = "death_delay",
                       time_last_obs = "report_delay",
                       xlim_days = c(0, 50),
                       ylim_surv = NULL,    
                       title_prefix = "Survival Curve: Onset → Death") {
  d <- build_surv_dataset(df, virus_label, group_var, time_event, time_last_obs, xlim_days)
  if (nrow(d) < 2 || dplyr::n_distinct(d$.grp) < 1) {
    message(paste0("[", virus_label, "] ", group_var, ": No Data, skip"))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  fit <- survfit(Surv(t_obs, status) ~ .grp, data = d)

  td <- broom::tidy(fit) %>%
    mutate(group = sub("^\\.grp=", "", strata)) %>%
    rename(time_days = time, surv_prob = estimate,
           lower_ci = conf.low, upper_ci = conf.high) %>%

    mutate(
      surv_prob = pmin(pmax(surv_prob, 0), 1),
      lower_ci  = pmin(pmax(lower_ci,  0), 1),
      upper_ci  = pmin(pmax(upper_ci,  0), 1)
    )

  base <- td %>%
    distinct(group) %>%
    mutate(time_days = 0, surv_prob = 1, lower_ci = 1, upper_ci = 1)
  td <- bind_rows(base, td) %>% arrange(group, time_days)

  pval_txt <- ""
  if (n_distinct(d$.grp) > 1) {
    lr <- survdiff(Surv(t_obs, status) ~ .grp, data = d)
    pval <- 1 - pchisq(lr$chisq, df = length(lr$n) - 1)
    pval_txt <- paste0(" (log-rank p = ", format.pval(pval, digits = 3, eps = 1e-4), ")")
  }

  ggplot(td, aes(x = time_days, y = surv_prob, color = group)) +
    geom_step(linewidth = 0.9) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim_days, expand = expansion(mult = 0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      limits  = if (is.null(ylim_surv)) c(0, 1) else ylim_surv,
      n.breaks = 6,
      labels  = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.01),  
      expand  = expansion(mult = 0)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(title_prefix, " by ", gsub("_", " ", group_var),
                     " (", virus_label, ")", pval_txt),
      x = "Days since symptom onset",
      y = "P(delay > t)",
      color = gsub("_", " ", group_var)
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}

km_data_by <- function(df, virus_label, group_var,
                       time_event = "death_delay",
                       time_last_obs = "report_delay",
                       xlim_days = c(0, 50)) {
  d <- build_surv_dataset(df, virus_label, group_var, time_event, time_last_obs, xlim_days)
  if (nrow(d) < 2 || dplyr::n_distinct(d$.grp) < 1) return(NULL)
  fit <- survfit(Surv(t_obs, status) ~ .grp, data = d)
  broom::tidy(fit) %>%
    mutate(group = sub("^\\.grp=", "", strata)) %>%
    rename(time_days = time, surv_prob = estimate,
           lower_ci = conf.low, upper_ci = conf.high)
}

```




```{r}
#overall incidence by year
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(scales)


nat_pop <- sum(df$`POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`, na.rm = TRUE)

inc_year_nat <- combined_all %>%
  filter(case_status == "Confirmed") %>%
  mutate(year = format(as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y")) %>%
  count(virus, year, name = "cases") %>%
  mutate(incidence_per_100k = 1e5 * cases / nat_pop)

ggplot(inc_year_nat, aes(x = year, y = incidence_per_100k, fill = virus)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Incidence per 100k by Year (Brazil, 2015–2025)",
    x = "Year", y = "Incidence (per 100k)", fill = "Virus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


#death age by year

death_age_year <- combined_all %>%
  mutate(year = format(as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y")) %>%
  filter(case_status == "Death", !is.na(age_group),
         year >= "2015", year <= "2025") %>%
  count(year, virus, age_group, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(year, virus) %>%
  mutate(percentage = 100 * n / sum(n)) %>% ungroup()

ggplot(death_age_year, aes(x = year, y = percentage, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ virus) +
  labs(title = "Age-Group Composition of Deaths per Year (2015–2025)",
       x = "Year", y = "Percentage of Deaths", fill = "Age Group") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


#overall death
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2)

nat_pop <- sum(df$`POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`, na.rm = TRUE)

deaths_year <- combined_all %>%
  mutate(year = format(as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y")) %>%
  filter(case_status == "Death", year >= "2015", year <= "2025") %>%
  count(virus, year, name = "deaths") %>%
  mutate(mortality_per_100k = 1e5 * deaths / nat_pop)

ggplot(deaths_year, aes(x = year, y = mortality_per_100k, fill = virus)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Mortality per 100k by Year (Brazil, 2015–2025)",
       x = "Year", y = "Deaths per 100k", fill = "Virus") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))



```



```{r}

  library(dplyr)
  library(lubridate)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)


date_min   <- as.Date("2015-01-01")
date_max   <- as.Date("2025-12-31")
cols       <- c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")
dodge_w    <- 12    
box_w      <- 12    
use_log_y  <- FALSE 


ensure_code_muni7 <- function(dat) {
  dat %>%
    mutate(code_muni6 = substr(as.character(ID_MUNICIP), 1, 6)) %>%
    left_join(muni_lookup, by = "code_muni6") %>%
    mutate(code_muni = as.character(code_muni))
}


Zika_w <- Zika_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN == 1, !is.na(DT_SIN_PRI)) %>%
  mutate(
    DT_SIN_PRI = as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI),
    week  = floor_date(DT_SIN_PRI, "week", week_start = 1),
    virus = "Zika"
  ) %>%
  filter(week >= date_min, week <= date_max) %>%
  ensure_code_muni7()

Chik_w <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), !is.na(DT_SIN_PRI)) %>%
  mutate(
    DT_SIN_PRI = as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI),
    week  = floor_date(DT_SIN_PRI, "week", week_start = 1),
    virus = "Chikungunya"
  ) %>%
  filter(week >= date_min, week <= date_max) %>%
  ensure_code_muni7()

weekly_raw <- bind_rows(Zika_w, Chik_w) %>%
  filter(!is.na(code_muni))


pop_muni <- df %>%
  transmute(code_muni = as.character(code_muni),
            POP = `POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`) %>%
  filter(!is.na(code_muni), !is.na(POP), POP > 0)

nat_pop <- sum(pop_muni$POP, na.rm = TRUE)

wk_muni <- weekly_raw %>%
  count(virus, week, code_muni, name = "cases") %>%
  left_join(pop_muni, by = "code_muni") %>%
  filter(!is.na(POP), POP > 0) %>%
  mutate(inc_100k = 1e5 * cases / POP) %>%

  group_by(virus, week) %>%
  filter(any(!is.na(inc_100k))) %>%
  ungroup()


wk_nat <- weekly_raw %>%
  count(virus, week, name = "cases_nat") %>%
  mutate(inc_nat_100k = 1e5 * cases_nat / nat_pop) %>%
  arrange(virus, week)


p <- ggplot() +

  geom_boxplot(
    data = wk_muni,
    aes(x = week, y = inc_100k, group = interaction(virus, week),
        fill = virus),
    position = position_dodge2(width = dodge_w, preserve = "single"),
    width = box_w,
    color = "black",      
    size = 0.25,
    outlier.size = 0.6,
    outlier.alpha = 0.6
  ) +

  geom_line(
    data = wk_nat,
    aes(x = week, y = inc_nat_100k, color = virus),
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "Virus") +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = cols, name = "Virus") +
  scale_x_date(
    breaks = seq(date_min, date_max, by = "1 year"),
    date_labels = "%Y",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Figure 1. Weekly Incidence per 100k (Brazil, 2015–2025)",
    subtitle = "Boxes = municipality incidence per week; Lines = national weekly incidence",
    x = "Week (2015–2025)",
    y = "Incidence per 100,000"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

if (isTRUE(use_log_y)) {
  p <- p + scale_y_continuous(
    trans = "log10",
    breaks = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100),
    labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.1)
  )
} else {
  p <- p + scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.1))
}

print(p)

#top weeks  for now its top 3
wk_nat %>%
  group_by(virus) %>%
  slice_max(inc_nat_100k, n = 3, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)


date_min <- as.Date("2015-01-01")
date_max <- as.Date("2025-12-31")
cols <- c("Zika" = "#66c2a5", "Chikungunya" = "#fc8d62")

# country level incidence
wk_nat <- weekly_raw %>%
  count(virus, week, name = "cases_nat") %>%
  mutate(inc_nat_100k = 1e5 * cases_nat / nat_pop) %>%
  filter(week >= date_min, week <= date_max)


p <- ggplot(wk_nat, aes(x = week, y = inc_nat_100k, color = virus)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "Virus") +
  scale_x_date(
    breaks = seq(date_min, date_max, by = "1 year"),
    date_labels = "%Y"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Figure 1. Weekly National Incidence per 100k (Brazil, 2015–2025)",
    x = "Week (2015–2025)",
    y = "Incidence per 100,000"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(p)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# =========================
# Time window
# =========================
date_min <- as.Date("2015-01-01")
date_max <- as.Date("2025-12-31")

# =========================
# PREP: weekly national chikungunya
# =========================
wk_chik <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), !is.na(DT_SIN_PRI)) %>%
  mutate(
    DT_SIN_PRI = as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI),
    week = floor_date(DT_SIN_PRI, "week", week_start = 1)
  ) %>%
  filter(week >= date_min, week <= date_max) %>%
  count(week, name = "cases_nat") %>%
  mutate(
    inc_nat_100k = 1e5 * cases_nat / nat_pop
  ) %>%
  arrange(week)

# =========================
# FIGURE 1: bars + line
# =========================
p <- ggplot(wk_chik, aes(x = week)) +

  # ---- weekly national cases (bars)
  geom_col(
    aes(y = cases_nat),
    fill  = "grey80",
    color = NA,
    width = 6
  ) +

  # ---- weekly incidence per 100k (line)
  geom_line(
    aes(y = inc_nat_100k * 1000),
    color = "#fc8d62",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +

  scale_x_date(
    breaks = seq(date_min, date_max, by = "1 year"),
    date_labels = "%Y",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.02))
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Weekly cases (national total)",
    labels = comma,
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ . / 1000,
      name = "Weekly incidence per 100,000"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Figure 1. Weekly chikungunya burden in Brazil, 2015–2025",
    x = "Year"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "#fc8d62"),
    axis.text.y.right  = element_text(color = "#fc8d62")
  )

print(p)




```

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# =========================
# Time window
# =========================
date_min <- as.Date("2015-01-01")
date_max <- as.Date("2025-12-31")

# =========================
# PREP: weekly national chikungunya
# =========================
wk_chik <- Chik_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13), !is.na(DT_SIN_PRI)) %>%
  mutate(
    DT_SIN_PRI = as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI),
    week = floor_date(DT_SIN_PRI, "week", week_start = 1)
  ) %>%
  filter(week >= date_min, week <= date_max) %>%
  count(week, name = "cases_nat") %>%
  mutate(
    inc_nat_100k = 1e5 * cases_nat / nat_pop,
    week_of_year = isoweek(week)
  ) %>%
  arrange(week)

# =========================
# BASELINE: long-term mean for same calendar week
# =========================
baseline <- wk_chik %>%
  group_by(week_of_year) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_inc = mean(inc_nat_100k, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

wk_chik <- wk_chik %>%
  left_join(baseline, by = "week_of_year") %>%
  mutate(
    deviation = inc_nat_100k - mean_inc
  )

# =========================
# Scaling factors (for shared plot space)
# =========================
scale_inc  <- 1000     # incidence → secondary axis
scale_dev  <- 1000     # deviation → secondary axis

# =========================
# FIGURE
# =========================
p <- ggplot(wk_chik, aes(x = week)) +

  # ---- Bars: weekly national cases
  geom_col(
    aes(y = cases_nat),
    fill = "grey80",
    width = 6
  ) +

  # ---- Line 1: weekly incidence
  geom_line(
    aes(y = inc_nat_100k * scale_inc),
    color = "#fc8d62",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +

  # ---- Line 2: deviation from weekly baseline
  geom_line(
    aes(y = deviation * scale_dev),
    color = "#d7301f",
    linewidth = 0.7,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +

  scale_x_date(
    breaks = seq(date_min, date_max, by = "1 year"),
    date_labels = "%Y",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.02))
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Weekly cases (national total)",
    labels = comma,
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ . / scale_inc,
      name = "Weekly incidence / deviation (per 100,000)"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Figure 1. Weekly chikungunya dynamics in Brazil, 2015–2025",
    subtitle = "Bars: national cases; Solid line: incidence; Dashed line: deviation from long-term weekly mean",
    x = "Year"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "#fc8d62")
  )

print(p)


```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(tibble)

# =========================
# 2022 Population Census
# =========================
race_pop <- tibble::tribble(
  ~race_label,        ~population,
  "White",             88252121,
  "Black",             20656458,
  "Asian",               850130,
  "Pardo (Mixed)",     92083286,
  "Indigenous",         1227642
)

# =========================
# Incidence per 100k by race/year (Chikungunya only)
# =========================
race_year_inc <- race_year_summary %>%
  filter(virus == "Chikungunya") %>%
  left_join(race_pop, by = "race_label") %>%
  filter(!is.na(population), population > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    incidence_per_100k = (n / population) * 100000
  )

# =========================
# Share of incidence (100% stacked)
# =========================
race_year_pct_inc <- race_year_inc %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  mutate(
    share = incidence_per_100k / sum(incidence_per_100k, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# =========================
# Plot
# =========================
ggplot(race_year_pct_inc,
       aes(x = year, y = share, fill = race_label)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill", color = "white", width = 0.9) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Incidence per 100k by Race/Ethnicity (Chikungunya, 2015–2025)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Share of incidence",
    fill = "Race / Ethnicity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )



```


Cleaned population 2022 census
```{r}


library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

gamdata<- read_excel("/Users/wuchangpengyuyan/Downloads/tabela9606.xlsx", sheet = "Tabela") %>% tidyr::fill(Race, .direction = "down")


```


Refined gam model using 2022 data


library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)
library(gratia)
library(ggplot2)


pop_df <- gamdata %>%
  rename(age_year = Age,
         race_label = Race,
         population = Population) %>%
  mutate(
    age_year = as.integer(age_year),
    race_label = recode(race_label,
                        "Pardo" = "Pardo (Mixed)")
  )


reg_df <- combined_all %>%
  filter(case_status == "Confirmed",
         !is.na(age_years), age_years <= 100,
         !is.na(race_label),
         !is.na(DT_SIN_PRI)) %>%
  mutate(
   race_label = recode(race_label, "Pardo" = "Pardo (Mixed)"),
    ym    = format(as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y-%m"),
    ym_id = as.integer(factor(ym, levels = sort(unique(ym)))),
    age_year  = pmin(100L, floor(age_years)),
    race_label = factor(race_label,
                        levels = c("White", "Pardo (Mixed)", "Black", "Indigenous", "Asian"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(virus, ym_id, age_year, race_label) %>%
  summarise(cases = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(pop_df, by = c("age_year", "race_label")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(population) & population > 0) %>%
  ungroup()



# Chikungunya
fit_chik_full <- gam(
  cases ~ s(ym_id, bs = "cs", k = 120) +
          s(age_year, bs = "cs", k = 20) +
          race_label +
          offset(log(population)),
  family = nb(link = "log"),
  data   = reg_df %>% filter(virus == "Chikungunya"),
  method = "REML"
)

# Zika
fit_zika_full <- gam(
  cases ~ s(ym_id, bs = "cs", k = 120) +
          s(age_year, bs = "cs", k = 20) +
          race_label +
          offset(log(population)),
  family = nb(link = "log"),
  data   = reg_df %>% filter(virus == "Zika"),
  method = "REML"
)


summary(fit_chik_full)
summary(fit_zika_full)

gam.check(fit_chik_full)
gam.check(fit_zika_full)

draw(fit_chik_full, residuals = FALSE) 
draw(fit_zika_full, residuals = FALSE)

# ------------------------------
# Model Prediction & Visualization (Fixed)
# ------------------------------
# ---- Prediction on observed data ----
reg_df <- reg_df %>%
  mutate(
    pred_chik = if_else(
      virus == "Chikungunya",
      predict(fit_chik_full, newdata = reg_df, type = "response"),
      NA_real_
    ),
    pred_zika = if_else(
      virus == "Zika",
      predict(fit_zika_full, newdata = reg_df, type = "response"),
      NA_real_
    ),
    resid_chik = if_else(virus == "Chikungunya", cases - pred_chik, NA_real_),
    resid_zika = if_else(virus == "Zika", cases - pred_zika, NA_real_)
  )

# =====================================================
# 6. Visualization 1: Observed vs Predicted (Zika)
# =====================================================
ggplot(reg_df %>% filter(virus == "Zika"),
       aes(x = cases, y = pred_zika, color = race_label)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Observed vs Predicted Zika Cases",
       x = "Observed Cases", y = "Predicted Cases") +
  theme_minimal()

# =====================================================
# 7. Visualization 2: Time-series Prediction with 95% CI
# =====================================================

pred_data <- reg_df %>%
  filter(virus == "Zika",
         age_year == 20,
         race_label == "Black") %>%
  distinct(ym_id, age_year, race_label, population, cases)

pred_result <- predict(
  fit_zika_full,
  newdata = pred_data,
  type = "link",
  se.fit = TRUE
)

pred_data <- pred_data %>%
  mutate(
    fit_link  = pred_result$fit,
    se_link   = pred_result$se.fit,
    pred_resp = exp(fit_link),
    lower_resp = exp(fit_link - 1.96 * se_link),
    upper_resp = exp(fit_link + 1.96 * se_link)
  )


ggplot(pred_data, aes(x = ym_id, y = pred_resp)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower_resp, ymax = upper_resp),
              fill = "orange", alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(color = "orange", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Predicted Chikungunya Cases Over Time (Age 30, Pardo Mixed)",
       x = "Month (ym_id)",
       y = "Predicted Cases (with 95% CI)") +
  theme_minimal()
"



The "INLA" Package
```{r}


# =======================================
# 1. Libraries
# =======================================
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyr)
library(geobr)
library(sf)
library(spdep)
library(INLA)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(scales)

# =======================================
# 2. Data setup
# =======================================



# ---- Population ----
pop_df <- df %>%
  transmute(
    code_muni = substr(as.character(code_muni), 1, 6),
    POP = as.numeric(`POPULAÇÃO ESTIMADA`)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(code_muni), !is.na(POP), POP > 0) %>%
  mutate(code_muni = str_pad(code_muni, width = 6, pad = "0"))
# ============================================================
# National monthly Zika incidence (Z_t)
# ============================================================

zika_nat_month <- Zika_total %>%
  filter(CLASSI_FIN == 1, !is.na(DT_SIN_PRI)) %>%
  mutate(
    ym = format(as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y-%m")
  ) %>%
  count(ym, name = "cases_zika") %>%
  left_join(
    pop_df %>% summarise(POP_nat = sum(POP)),
    by = character()
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Z_t_raw = cases_zika / POP_nat * 1e5, # incidence per 100k,
    Z_t = as.numeric(scale(Z_t_raw))
  ) %>%
  select(ym, Z_t)

# ---- Municipality shapefile (REFERENCE ORDER for graph) ----
muni_sf <- read_municipality(year = 2020) %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%
  mutate(code_muni = substr(as.character(code_muni), 1, 6)) %>%
  mutate(code_muni = str_pad(code_muni, width = 6, pad = "0"))

# This is the ONLY valid ordering for BYM2 graph indexing
space_levels <- muni_sf$code_muni
stopifnot(length(space_levels) == nrow(muni_sf))

# ---- Build adjacency graph ----
nb <- poly2nb(muni_sf)
adj_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".adj")
nb2INLA(file = adj_file, nb = nb)
g <- INLA::inla.read.graph(adj_file)

# =======================================
# 3. Functions
# =======================================

# ---- Municipality × Month panel (GRAPH-ALIGNED)
# IMPORTANT: This builds incidence model with offset = log(POP)
make_panel_muni_month_pop <- function(dat, pop_df, space_levels, zika_nat_month) {

  panel <- dat %>%
    transmute(
      code_muni = substr(as.character(ID_MUNICIP), 1, 6),
      code_muni = str_pad(code_muni, width = 6, pad = "0"),
      ym = format(as.Date(DT_SIN_PRI), "%Y-%m"),
      is_conf = CLASSI_FIN %in% c(1, 13)
    ) %>%
    filter(is_conf, !is.na(code_muni), !is.na(ym)) %>%
    count(code_muni, ym, name = "cases") %>%
    complete(code_muni, ym, fill = list(cases = 0L)) %>%
    left_join(pop_df, by = "code_muni") %>%
    filter(!is.na(POP), POP > 0) %>%
    filter(code_muni %in% space_levels) %>%
    mutate(
      space = match(code_muni, space_levels),
      time  = as.integer(factor(ym, levels = sort(unique(ym))))
    )

  # ---- 加 Z_t（national Zika incidence）----
  panel <- panel %>%
    left_join(zika_nat_month, by = "ym") %>%
    mutate(
      Z_t = ifelse(is.na(Z_t), 0, Z_t)
    )

  # ---- space–time index ----
  n_space <- length(space_levels)
  panel <- panel %>%
    mutate(
      st_id = (time - 1L) * n_space + space,
      offset_pop = log(POP)
    )

  stopifnot(!anyNA(panel$Z_t))
  stopifnot(!anyNA(panel$space))
  stopifnot(!anyNA(panel$time))
  stopifnot(!anyNA(panel$offset_pop))

  panel
}


fit_inla_space_time_full_interact_pop <- function(panel_df, graph) {

  formula <- cases ~
     Z_t + 
    f(space, model = "bym2", graph = graph, scale.model = TRUE,
      hyper = list(
        prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(1, 0.01)),
        phi  = list(prior = "pc",      param = c(0.5, 2/3))
      )) +
    f(time, model = "rw2",
      hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(1, 0.01))),
      constr = TRUE) +
    f(st_id, model = "iid",
      hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(2, 0.01))))

  inla(
    formula,
    family = "poisson",
    data = panel_df,
    offset = panel_df$offset_pop,   # <-- log(POP)
    control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1),
    control.compute = list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE),
    num.threads = "1:1",
    verbose = TRUE
  )
}



# ---- Extract TOTAL log(rate) where rate = mu / POP = exp(eta) ----
extract_total_lograte <- function(fit_obj, panel_df, per = 1e5) {

  alpha <- fit_obj$summary.fixed["(Intercept)", "mean"]
  beta  <- fit_obj$summary.fixed["Z_t", "mean"]
  spatial <- fit_obj$summary.random$space %>%
    transmute(space = ID, sp = mean)

  temporal <- fit_obj$summary.random$time %>%
    transmute(time = ID, tm = mean)

  interact <- fit_obj$summary.random$st_id %>%
    transmute(st_id = ID, it = mean)

  out <- panel_df %>%
    distinct(code_muni, ym, space, time, st_id, POP, Z_t) %>%
    left_join(spatial,  by = "space") %>%
    left_join(temporal, by = "time") %>%
    left_join(interact, by = "st_id") %>%
    mutate(
      eta = alpha + beta * Z_t + sp + tm + it,
      log_rate_per_person = eta,
      rate_per_person = exp(eta),
      # scale to per 100k (or per=1e5)
      log_rate_per = eta + log(per),
      rate_per = exp(eta) * per,
      ym_date = as.Date(paste0(ym, "-01"))
    )

  out
}



# =======================================
# 4. Build panel (Chik only) + sanity checks
# =======================================

panel_chik_full <- make_panel_muni_month_pop(
  Chik_total,
  pop_df,
  space_levels,
  zika_nat_month
)

#fit_chik_interact_pop <- fit_inla_space_time_full_interact_pop(panel_chik_full,g)


#saveRDS(fit_chik_interact_pop, "~/Desktop/fit_chik_interact_pop.rds")

# Load existing
fit_chik_interact_pop <- readRDS("~/Desktop/fit_chik_interact_pop.rds")

# Extract log(rate)
st_rate_chik <- extract_total_lograte(fit_chik_interact_pop, panel_chik_full, per = 1e5)

# =======================================

st_rate_chik <- st_rate_chik %>%
  left_join(
    pop_df %>% select(code_muni, POP),
    by = "code_muni"
  )

# ============================================================
# Plotting (IRR relative to median)
# ASSUMES:
#   st_rate_chik: municipality × time, contains
#     - rate_per   (incidence per 100k)
#     - ym_date
#     - POP
#     - code_muni
#     - Z_t
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# 1. National population-weighted median Incidence
# ============================================================
nat_rate_median <- st_rate_chik %>%
  group_by(ym_date) %>%
  summarise(
    nat_rate = median(rate_per, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )



# ============================================================
# 2. Attach region + compute IRR
# ============================================================
state_region_lookup <- tibble(
 code_state = sprintf("%02d", c(
 11,12,13,14,15,16,17,
 21,22,23,24,25,26,27,28,29,
 31,32,33,35,
 41,42,43,
 50,51,52,53
 )),
 region = c(
 rep("North", 7),
 rep("Northeast", 9),
 rep("Southeast", 4),
 rep("South", 3),
rep("Center-West", 4)
 )
 )


st_rate_chik_region <- st_rate_chik %>%
  mutate(code_state = substr(code_muni, 1, 2)) %>%
  left_join(state_region_lookup, by = "code_state") %>%
  filter(!is.na(region)) %>%
  left_join(nat_rate_median, by = "ym_date") %>%
  mutate(
    IRR     = rate_per / nat_rate,
    log_IRR = log(IRR)
  )

st_rate_chik_region$region <- factor(
  st_rate_chik_region$region,
  levels = c("North", "Northeast", "Center-West", "Southeast", "South")
)


# ============================================================
# 3. Macro-region × month summaries (IRR)
# ============================================================
region_time_irr <- st_rate_chik_region %>%
  group_by(region, ym_date) %>%
  summarise(
    median_logIRR = median(log_IRR, na.rm = TRUE),
    frac_high     = mean(IRR > 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


# ============================================================
# 4. Macro-region × year summaries (IRR)
# ============================================================
region_year_irr <- st_rate_chik_region %>%
  mutate(year = year(ym_date)) %>%
  group_by(region, year) %>%
  summarise(
    median_logIRR = median(log_IRR, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


# ============================================================
# 5. Heatmap — Monthly log(IRR)
# ============================================================

p_region_heat_month <- ggplot(
  region_time_irr,
  aes(x = ym_date, y = region, fill = median_logIRR)
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#0033FF",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#FF0000",
    midpoint = 0,
    name = "log(IRR)\n(vs national median)"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +
  labs(
    title = "Chikungunya Relative Incidence by Macro-region (Monthly)",
    subtitle = "Relative to national municipality-median incidence",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Region"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_region_heat_month)


# ============================================================
# 6. Heatmap — Annual log(IRR)
# ============================================================

p_region_heat_year <- ggplot(
  region_year_irr,
  aes(x = year, y = region, fill = median_logIRR)
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#0033FF",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#FF0000",
    midpoint = 0,
    name = "log(IRR)\n(vs national median)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(region_year_irr$year),
                                  max(region_year_irr$year), 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Chikungunya Relative Incidence by Macro-region (Annual)",
    subtitle = "Relative to national municipality-median incidence",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Region"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_region_heat_year)


# ============================================================
# 7. Line plot — Temporal trends (log IRR)
# ============================================================
p_region_line <- ggplot(
  region_time_irr,
  aes(x = ym_date, y = median_logIRR, color = region)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "2 year", date_labels = "%Y") +
  labs(
    title = "Temporal Trends in Relative Incidence by Macro-region",
    subtitle = "Relative to national municipality-median incidence",
    x = "Year",
    y = "log(IRR)",
    color = "Region"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_region_line)


# ============================================================
# 8. Fraction of municipalities with IRR > 1
# ============================================================
p_region_frac <- ggplot(
  region_time_irr,
  aes(x = ym_date, y = frac_high, color = region)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "2 year", date_labels = "%Y") +
  labs(
    title = "Fraction of Municipalities Above National Median Incidence",
    subtitle = "IRR > 1",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Fraction",
    color = "Region"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_region_frac)


#IR ungrouped municipality map

# ============================================================
# Municipality × time heatmap (ABSOLUTE incidence)
# ============================================================

q <- quantile(st_rate_chik$log_rate_per, c(0.01, 0.99), na.rm = TRUE)

p_muni_abs <- ggplot(
  st_rate_chik,
  aes(x = ym_date, y = code_muni, fill = log_rate_per)
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "grey",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#FF0000",
    midpoint = median(st_rate_chik$log_rate_per, na.rm = TRUE),
    limits = q,
    oob = squish,
    name = "log(rate / 100k)"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +
  labs(
    title = "Chikungunya Incidence Rate Across Municipalities",
    subtitle = "Absolute incidence rate estimated from spatial–temporal model",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Municipality"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

print(p_muni_abs)




#same but by latitude order

muni_lat <- muni_sf %>%
  mutate(
    pt  = st_point_on_surface(geom),
    lat = st_coordinates(pt)[, 2]
  ) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(code_muni, lat)

st_rate_chik_lat <- st_rate_chik %>%
  left_join(muni_lat, by = "code_muni") %>%
  filter(!is.na(lat))
summary(st_rate_chik_lat$lat)

muni_order_lat <- st_rate_chik_lat %>%
  distinct(code_muni, lat) %>%
  arrange(lat) %>%          # south → north
  pull(code_muni)

st_rate_chik_lat <- st_rate_chik_lat %>%
  mutate(
    code_muni_lat = factor(code_muni, levels = muni_order_lat)
  )


p_muni_abs_lat <- ggplot(
  st_rate_chik_lat,
  aes(x = ym_date, y = code_muni_lat, fill = log_rate_per)
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
```
